narrative.headline: Steering leads the 2018 Toyota 4Runner complaints, but no single issue dominates
narrative.summary: Owners filed 57 complaints with NHTSA, spread fairly evenly across several systems. Steering is the most reported area at 11 reports (19%), followed by air bags at 7 (12%) and service brakes at 6 (11%). The air bag complaints stand out on safety: 6 of the 7 involved a crash and 1 reported an injury. Structure complaints include 2 crashes. The other top areas report no crashes or fires. Pay closest attention to the steering, brakes, and air bag system on inspection.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 11 reports (19% of all complaints), with no crashes or fires. Check steering feel and any pulling or vibration on a test drive.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 7 reports (12%) and the most safety-flagged area, with 6 crashes and 1 injury among them. Confirm the air bag system has no warning lights and a clean history.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 6 reports (11%), with no crashes or fires. Test the brake pedal firmness and stopping distance before buying.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 5 reports (9%), with no crashes or fires. Listen for unusual noises and watch for any loss of power on a test drive.

issues.blurb: 5 reports (9%), including 2 crashes. Inspect body panels and trim for fit and any separation.
